mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing three months signals an active tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the policy rate keeps climbing.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage_value 2.56 in Sept 2022 reflecting active hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during rising-rate periods are not statistically more negative than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points from its 12-month trailing low, this Sahm-rule-like threshold signals the onset of labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.5% in Sept 2022, near cycle lows, trigger not yet active
failure_condition: When triggered, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the signal fails to precede an NBER-dated recession within 12 months in the majority of triggers.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS exceeding trailing CPIAUCSL-implied inflation (i.e., a rising real policy rate) signals that monetary policy has become restrictive in real terms, amplifying downside pressure on equity valuations beyond what either series predicts alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL YoY inflation turns positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 2.56% vs elevated CPI level 296.76 in Sept 2022 vintage
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over the univariate FEDFUNDS trend signal (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10 in walk-forward regression), or joint-triggered periods do not underperform single-signal periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST during a monetary tightening regime signals that higher mortgage rates are suppressing housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer retrenchment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days specifically when FEDFUNDS is above 2%.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1531k in Aug 2022 vintage, declining from 1575k prior reading
failure_condition: During periods when HOUST declines but FEDFUNDS < 2%, forward 60d returns are equally negative, indicating the regime conditioning adds no discriminatory power (difference in means not significant at p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects resilient industrial output and supports near-term corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as the market prices continued production strength.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 101.10 in Aug 2022 vintage, down from initial 104.55 reading, indicating vintage sensitivity
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly trend and forward 5d returns stays below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the sign of the correlation flips across more than two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth decelerates below its trailing 6-month average by more than one standard deviation, this signals weakening consumer spending momentum that leads to downward revisions in corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth falls more than 1 std dev below its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 661854 vs vintage 683974 in Sept 2022, showing downward revision risk
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive forward 20d returns more than 55% of the time, or the deceleration threshold fails to precede any measurable slowdown in subsequent GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of decelerating PAYEMS growth alongside a still-rising FEDFUNDS signals that the labor market is beginning to crack even as policy remains restrictive, an unstable combination that precedes sharper equity drawdowns than either signal alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1% while FEDFUNDS rises YoY
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised value 153582k vs vintage 153018k in Sept 2022 showing modest ongoing job growth
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle nearing labor market turning point
failure_condition: Joint-triggered periods do not underperform periods triggered by PAYEMS deceleration alone (difference in mean 60d returns not significant at p<0.10), rejecting the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL month-over-month print that exceeds consensus-implied trend by accelerating above its trailing 3-month average signals persistent inflation pressure that raises the probability of more aggressive Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as rate-hike expectations are repriced upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeds its trailing 3-month average by more than 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value 296.761 vs revised 296.349 in Sept 2022, indicating elevated and volatile inflation readings
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Triggered CPI surprise events do not produce forward 5d returns significantly more negative than non-triggered periods (t-test p>0.05), or the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS falls below 1%.
